findings.description: assemble iterates range(total_chunks - 1), dropping the final chunk and producing a truncated/corrupt output file.
Client-supplied session.filename is joined into UPLOAD_DIR without sanitization, allowing path traversal or absolute-path arbitrary file write (e.g. '../../etc/...').
upload_id is interpolated unsanitized into chunk file paths, enabling path traversal for chunk write/read/delete outside CHUNK_DIR.
Chunk read path during assemble is built from unsanitized upload_id, inheriting the same traversal exposure as the write path.
_cleanup_chunks deletes paths built from unsanitized upload_id, allowing crafted ids to remove files outside CHUNK_DIR.
Extension allowlist checks only the final suffix with no MIME/magic-byte validation, so content type is unverified and easily spoofed (double extensions).
verify_checksum reads the entire file into memory via f.read(); with MAX_FILE_MB=200 this loads up to 200MB per call instead of streaming in blocks.
MAX_FILE_MB is never enforced during receive_chunk or assemble, so an attacker can write unbounded chunk data to disk (disk-exhaustion DoS); file_size_ok is advisory and never called by assemble.
start() overwrites an existing session for the same upload_id without cleaning previously received chunk files, orphaning them on disk.
assemble opens each chunk path without checking existence; a missing chunk file raises an unhandled FileNotFoundError mid-write, leaving a partial dest file.
Final file is written in place rather than to a temp path with atomic rename, so a crash mid-assembly leaves a corrupt partial file at dest_path.
Writes/reads follow symlinks at dest_path/chunk paths without checking for regular files, allowing a symlink to redirect the write or read.
Checksum compared with non-constant-time '!=' rather than hmac.compare_digest (low risk for an integrity digest).
is_complete trusts the in-memory received set and does not verify chunk files still exist on disk, so completeness can be falsely true after external deletion.
No locking around assemble/abort/session mutation; concurrent calls on the same upload_id can race into double-assembly or KeyError on session deletion.
Chunk writes are not flushed/fsynced, so a crash after receive_chunk returns True may lose data that is_complete believes is present.
typing.Optional could be replaced with the modern 'str | None' union syntax for the 3.10+ target implied by set[int]/dict[str, ...] annotations.
